Our Services

Our SMETA Audit Services

Gap Assessment

Documentation Support

Score Optimization

Audit Preparation & Support

Gap Assessment

A comprehensive review of your existing policies, procedures, records, and operational practices against SMETA requirements.

Compliance Gap Analysis

Identify weaknesses before the audit.

Risk Prioritization

Focus on high-impact improvement areas.

Readiness Evaluation

Understand your current compliance maturity.

![SMETA Audit](https://growlity @media (max-width: 767px) { } /* 2. Inner Flex Layout & Gap */ /* 3. Button Wrapper Alignment */ /* 4. Button Mobile Padding */ @media (max-width: 767px) { } /* 5. Section Title Typography */

Understanding

SMETA Audit Pillars

Labour Standards

Health & Safety

Environment

Business Ethics

Labour Standards

Assessment of employee welfare, wages, working hours, contracts, and worker rights.

Key Focus Areas

  • Child labour prevention
  • Forced labour prevention
  • Fair wages
  • Working hours compliance
  • Freedom of association

![SMETA Audit](https://growlity @media (max-width: 767px) { } /* 2. Inner Flex Layout & Gap */ /* 3. Button Wrapper Alignment */ /* 4. Button Mobile Padding */ @media (max-width: 767px) { } /* 5. Section Title Typography */

Our 5-Step SMETA Audit Preparation Process

1

Step 1: Initial Assessment

Understand your business operations, risks, and compliance status.

2

Step 2: Gap Analysis

Benchmark current practices against SMETA requirements.

3

Step 3: Action Plan Development

Prioritize corrective actions and implementation measures.

4

Step 4: Documentation & Training

Prepare evidence, policies, records, and employee awareness programs.

5

Step 5: Audit Readiness Review

Conduct a final review to maximize audit preparedness.

Testimonials

On The Side of Ambitious

 Action 

FAQs

On The Side of Ambitious

 Action 

We help brands stand out and grow by creating innovative, results-driven strategies that engage customers and drive success.

What is the difference between SMETA and SEDEX?

SEDEX is a platform for sharing supplier information, while SMETA is the audit methodology used to assess ethical compliance.

How long does a SMETA Audit take?

The duration depends on company size, workforce, and operational complexity.

Who needs a SMETA Audit?

Manufacturers, exporters, suppliers, and organizations working with global brands often require a SMETA Audit.

What is covered in a SMETA 4-Pillar Audit?

Labor Standards, Health & Safety, Environment, and Business Ethics.

How can we prepare for a SMETA Audit?

A gap assessment, documentation review, employee training, and corrective action implementation significantly improve readiness.

Is SMETA Audit mandatory?

It is not legally mandatory but is frequently required by customers and procurement teams.

How often should a SMETA Audit be conducted?

Most organizations undergo audits based on customer requirements, typically every 1–3 years.

Can you help with corrective action plans?

Yes. We provide end-to-end support for corrective action implementation and continuous improvement.

Explore More

You might also Be Interested In…!!!

ESG Reporting

ESG Reporting refers to the structured disclosure of a company’s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance. It enables businesses to demonstrate how they manage risks and opportunities across climate change, resource efficiency, human rights, ethical governance, diversity, and more. In today’s investor-driven, regulation-focused environment, ESG reporting is no longer optional—it’s a strategic imperative. ESG disclosures influence investment decisions, consumer trust, regulatory compliance, and corporate reputation.

Read More

ESG Reporting

Net Zero u0026 Decarbonisation

Net Zero is no longer a distant ambition, it’s a business-critical commitment. At Growlity, we help organizations chart their Net Zero transformation with robust GHG accounting, practical decarbonisation strategies, and alignment with frameworks like the GHG Protocol, ISO 14064, SBTi, and CDP. Whether you’re just beginning your decarbonisation journey or already setting net zero targets, our sustainability consultants deliver tailored solutions that are strategic, science-based, and results-driven.

Read More

Net Zero u0026 Decarbonisation

CDP Reporting

CDP is a globally recognized environmental disclosure system that enables organizations to report their carbon emissions, climate-related risks, and environmental performance. Trusted by thousands of investors, companies, and cities, CDP plays a critical role in driving climate transparency and accountability. As a CDP consultant and disclosure partner, Growlity empowers organizations to navigate the full CDP reporting framework, enhance data quality, and improve scores. Our expert support ensures climate compliance, strengthens sustainability disclosures, and reinforces stakeholder trust—positioning your business for credible, low-carbon growth.

Read More

CDP Reporting
